title: Coulomb constant
short: The proportionality constant in Coulomb's law, k ≈ 8.99 × 10⁹ N·m²/C².
full:
The Coulomb constant k sets the strength of the electrostatic force in Coulomb's law, F = k·q₁·q₂/r². Its large value, k ≈ 8.99 × 10⁹ N·m²/C², tells you the electric force between everyday charges is enormous compared with gravity — which is why bulk matter is almost perfectly neutral.

The calculator substitutes k for you. It is related to the permittivity of free space by k = 1/(4πε₀).

The SI unit is N·m²/C².
